name: Transit
content:
Tuscaloosa is not as car dependent as other Alabama cities. It is possible to run most errands by foot or by bike. Tuscaloosa has an Amtrak station connecting it to New Orleans, Birmingham, Atlanta, and by extension, New York, though all routes are rather slow and I have never tried the train myself. Tuscaloosa and the university both have a bus system, though I have not tried out either. Unfortunately, neither bus system shows up on Google maps.
The river walk runs along the Black Warrior river, providing both a nice place to go for a walk, as well as a pedestrian and cycling corridor between the university and downtown Tuscaloosa.
I had a long talk with a woman who grew up in Tuscaloosa during the great depression. Tuscaloosa was much more walking and bus friendly back then. When she returned in the 1960’s, almost all non-car infrastructure was gone. It hurts a little to know that a better life existed, and it got torn down within the lifetimes of those who can remember.
